prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-UR7wak.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.task: Add user notification preferences model, preferences service, integrate with notification service to check preferences before sending email notifications, and add tests

8. All files in src/config/ - configuration
9. The test structure in tests/

Read ALL of these files completely. I need the full content of every file to understand the architecture, patterns, interfaces, and conventions used. Be very thorough - read every single file in the project.

alternatives.option: Opt-in model: require preferences to exist before sending
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would break existing notification flow for all current users; requires migration to create preference records for every user

alternatives.option: Constructor injection of PreferencesService into NotificationService
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would break existing constructor signature and all existing tests; setter injection is less disruptive while achieving the same goal

alternatives.option: Store preferences directly in NotificationService without a separate service
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates repository pattern used throughout the codebase; preferences need their own data access layer for consistency

assumptions: Email is currently the only notification channel
Future sessions may add more channels
Users should receive all notifications by default until they opt out
parameters.context: Need to add user notification preferences so users can control email notification settings (toggle, categories, frequency, quiet hours). Must integrate with existing event-driven notification architecture without breaking existing code.
parameters.rationale: Opt-out model (send by default, users must explicitly disable) ensures backwards compatibility — existing users receive all notifications without needing preferences created. PreferencesService is injected into NotificationService via setPreferencesService() rather than constructor injection to keep the existing constructor signature and avoid breaking all existing tests. The notification service checks preferences before sending when a PreferencesService is registered; without one, all notifications pass through as before.
parameters.summary: User notification preferences use opt-out model with optional PreferencesService injection into NotificationService

parameters.detail:
Completed work:
1. MODEL: Created src/models/user-preferences.ts with UserPreferences interface, EmailFrequency type, QuietHours interface, and defaults.
2. REPOSITORY: Created src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts extending BaseRepository with findByUserId().
3. SERVICE: Created src/services/preferences.service.ts with CRUD operations, shouldSendEmail() check, subscribe/unsubscribe helpers, and resetToDefaults().
4. INTEGRATION: Modified src/services/notification.service.ts to accept optional PreferencesService via setPreferencesService() and check preferences before sending email notifications.
5. EXPORTS: Updated src/index.ts with all new types, services, and constants.
6. TESTS: Created tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts with 33 tests covering all CRUD operations, shouldSendEmail logic (opt-out model, category filtering, quiet hours with wrap-around), and NotificationService integration.

All 122 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

Key design decisions:
- Opt-out model: no preferences = send everything
- Optional PreferencesService injection preserves backwards compatibility
- Quiet hours support wrap-around (e.g., 22:00-07:00)
- Email frequency field (immediate/daily-digest/weekly-digest) is stored but not enforced at delivery time — future work to implement digest batching
parameters.summary: User notification preferences implemented: model, repository, service, notification integration, 33 tests passing
